title: Mortality from CVD, cancer, diabetes or CRD between exact ages 30 and 70 (%)
definition: Mortality from CVD, cancer, diabetes or CRD is the percent of 30-year-old-people who would die before their 70th birthday from any of cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes,  or chronic respiratory disease, assuming that s/he would experience current mortality rates at every age and s/he would not die from any other cause of death (e.g., injuries or HIV/AIDS).
unit: percent probability of dying between ages 30 and 70

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: World Health Organization, Global Health Observatory Data Repository (http://apps.who.int/ghodata/)., World Health Organization (WHO), uri: https://www.who.int/data/gho

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Solomon Islands, Fiji, Vanuatu, while the lightest include Korea, Rep., Switzerland, Israel; that contrast is the fastest way to read the ncd premature mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Premature mortality from noncommunicable diseases creates a fair health-transition puzzle with broad global coverage.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Life expectancy can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Air pollution mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Particulate emission damage can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 promotion: broad source-valid chronic-disease mortality map with a clear health-transition story.
Daily-worthy when not paired too closely with adult mortality or life-expectancy maps.
